mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate held near the zero lower bound reduces the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, supporting a persistent upward drift in equity prices as long as FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25% and does not begin a sustained rise. This mechanism is always active regardless of other conditions.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.09% as of 2014-11-01 vintage, near zero lower bound since 2008
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d SPX return falls below 0.02 over any rolling 3-year window, or positive-return rate during low-rate periods is not statistically higher than the unconditional base rate (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points over a rolling 3-month window signals deteriorating labor market conditions that historically precede risk-off equity repricing, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days after the threshold is crossed.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.8% as of 2014-11-01, near cycle lows
failure_condition: Among all triggered instances of a >0.3pp 3-month UNRATE rise, forward 20d SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of cases, or the effect is not significant at p < 0.10 across the full sample.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in industrial production and retail sales indicates synchronized supply-side and demand-side expansion, generating a durable positive earnings revision cycle that lifts S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon. The effect requires both INDPRO and RSAFS to show positive 3-month momentum simultaneously; neither series alone is sufficient because divergence between production and consumption signals imbalance rather than genuine expansion.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change > 0 AND RSAFS 3-month change > 0 simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage 106.66 vs RSAFS vintage 449282 as of late-2014 snapshot showing continued growth
failure_condition: Rank IC of the joint INDPRO+RSAFS momentum signal against forward 60d returns is not meaningfully higher than either single-series signal's IC, or falls below 0.03 in more than half of rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: During a zero-lower-bound monetary regime, rising housing starts reflect strengthening interest-rate-sensitive demand that historically leads broader consumption and equity performance, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns; this relationship is expected to weaken or reverse once monetary policy shifts to a tightening cycle, as housing strength then signals overheating risk instead of recovery.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST revised value 1074k units with FEDFUNDS at 0.09% as of late-2014
failure_condition: The positive association between HOUST growth and forward 60d SPX returns disappears or reverses sign once FEDFUNDS is held constant near zero, or the relationship fails to persist into a tightening regime test (out-of-sample rank IC < 0).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payroll employment reflects real-time labor demand strength that is quickly priced into near-term equity sentiment, producing a short-horizon positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days following a payroll release showing positive growth.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS vintage 140,045k vs revised 140,089k as of 2014-11-01 showing continued job growth
failure_condition: Average 5-day forward SPX return following positive PAYEMS monthly growth is not statistically distinguishable from average return following flat or negative growth (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When headline CPI year-over-year inflation crosses above 2.5%, markets begin pricing an accelerated pace of future rate hikes, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days after the threshold breach.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL revised value 237.43 as of 2014-10-01, YoY inflation below 2% at decision date
failure_condition: In periods where CPI YoY exceeds 2.5%, forward 20d SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the negative mean return is not significant at p < 0.10 relative to the unconditional baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in real GDP growth and retail sales growth confirms a broad-based demand expansion that supports sustained corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon. The interaction is required because RSAFS alone is noisy at monthly frequency while GDPC1 alone is too infrequent and revision-prone to time entries; their joint confirmation reduces false positives from either series individually.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth > 2% AND RSAFS 3-month change > 0
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 revised value 18406.941B vs vintage 16164.1B reflecting large revision risk as of 2014-07-01
failure_condition: Forward 60d returns conditioned on joint GDPC1/RSAFS expansion show no significant improvement in hit rate or Rank IC versus RSAFS-only signal across at least two independent 3-year out-of-sample windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, a month-over-month decline in industrial production signals emerging supply-side weakness that markets interpret as an early recession indicator, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days; this signal is expected to be uninformative or reversed during early-cycle recovery when INDPRO declines reflect residual slack rather than genuine contraction.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO revised value 103.7126 as of 2014-11-01 vintage during ongoing expansion
failure_condition: During late-cycle periods, forward 5d SPX returns following an INDPRO monthly decline are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the mean negative return is not significant at p < 0.10.
